channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: This volunteer crew is on a mission to fix Wikipedia’s worst celebrity photos
item.description: Visit a celebrity’s Wikipedia page and there’s a good chance you’ll be greeted by a blurry, outdated, or unflattering photo. These images often look like they were snapped in passing at a public event—because, in many cases, they were.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Cisco’s chief product officer explains the company’s transformation around AI
item.description: For 40 years, Cisco has been best known for building routers, switches, and other networking technology that connects computers within offices, data centers, and across the internet.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Huawei at center of European Parliament bribery investigation
item.description: Huawei is at the center of an investigation into alleged bribery and corruption within the European Parliament. Belgian Federal Police raided 21 locations across Belgium and in Portugal on Thursday morning and made arrests in Belgium and France as part of the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 40% of IT leaders scared to admit mistakes due to workplace culture of fear
item.description: New research claims many IT leaders are scared to admit mistakes, leading to potentially “major IT incident[s].
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: VergeIO enhances VergeFabric network virtualization offering
item.description: VergeIO is looking to shake up the virtual infrastructure market with its approach to virtualization and software-defined networking. VergeIO is not a startup; the company was founded in 2010 and hasn’t made huge waves in the market.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Today’s Steam Spring Sale features my absolute favorite game of all time - here's when the sale starts and all the key info
item.description: Here's the Steam Spring Sale 2025 start times for your timezone, and some predictions as to what we'd like to see discounted.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The latest iPhone 17 Pro Max leak may have given us another look at its upcoming redesign
item.description: We've got some more evidence showing the upcoming redesign – and it sounds as though production is on schedule.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Scopes monkey trial took place 100 years ago, but the fight isn't over
item.description: Tennessee became the first state in the country a hundred years ago to ban the teaching of evolution in public school classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Majority of Americans have used AI models like ChatGPT: Survey
item.description: A majority of Americans have used ChatGPT-like artificial intelligence (AI) models according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Google reportedly looking at smart glasses
item.description: After abandoning its Google Glass smart glasses in 2023, Google seems ready to take another kick at the can with what will reportedly be a $115 million acquisition of Canadian startup AdHawk Microsystems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok’s comment sections are being flooded with copy-pasted Christian messages
item.description: An influx of copy-and-pasted Christian messages has recently taken over TikTok’s comment sections. Over the past several days, comments about Jesus Christ have surfaced among the top comments on a wide range of unrelated videos.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: More than 10,000 domains registered for smishing campaign: Report
item.description: Cybercriminals have registered more than 10,000 domains for various smishing scams, according to a recent report from the threat intelligence arm at cybersecurity firm Palo Alto Networks.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Laser-wielding robots are redefining farming
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft’s plan for genAI profits: Squeeze customers
item.description: Microsoft has ridden its multibillion-dollar investments in generative AI (genAI) to become the world’s second-most valuable company, with a valuation of roughly $3 trillion, depending on the day’s stock price.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Altera targets low-latency AI edge applications with new FPGA products
item.description: Altera has introduced the latest family of Agilex FPGAs, along with Quartus Prime Pro software, and FPGA AI Suite to enable the rapid development of highly customized embedded systems for use in robotics, factory automation systems, and medical equipment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence
item.description: The U.S. Copyright Office completed the second of two studies of AI and copyrights as it dealt with whether you can copyright outputs created using AI. The post The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Hey Apple! When you’re in a hole, stop digging
item.description: Soon after the non-appearance of the most important Apple Intelligence feature, Apple now finds itself in danger of digging an even deeper hole by promising iOS 19 will be the most significant upgrade in years. The problem: it again runs the risk of promising too much and not delivering enough.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get more than $25M
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Weaviate adds agents to its tech stack to ease gen AI app development
item.description: Vector database provider Weaviate has added three new agents to its development stack to facilitate the development of generative AI-based applications. These agents are modular agentic workflows that use large language models (LLMs) and prompts to interact dynamically with data in Weaviate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2
item.description: Joe Welch, the deputy to the commanding general of the Army Futures Command, said the characteristics of need helps define the problem set for industry. The post How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
